behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: This is mainly a module-handoff/wiring risk (request builder -> API client). Unit tests on a formatter can miss dropped fields during integration. Rejected System because the behavior text is about request construction content, not a required real external response/round-trip side effect.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real request-construction path to a test double transport/client, invoke classification, and assert the outbound payload contains the full taxonomy label list for multiple taxonomies.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is a deterministic normalization/validation rule on returned label values. No real infra, subprocess, or model-quality dependence is required to expose the failure.
behaviors.test_description: Call the normalization/mapping logic with an out-of-taxonomy label (e.g., "medical") and assert the final returned label is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This is pure determinism/idempotence of a local normalization function given identical input. Higher-level checks add little new reality signal for this specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Feed the same raw API response object/string repeatedly into normalization and assert identical final labels across runs.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: The behavior explicitly depends on repeated live AI API outcomes and potential model/provider variability. A stub cannot validate real-model consistency behavior. Rejected Workflow because calls are repeated but not a multi-step agent pipeline with sequential model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run multiple real provider calls with identical document+taxonomy, store both raw provider responses and final labels, and assert labels stay consistent whenever raw provider responses are equivalent (allowing divergence only when provider response differs).

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Risk is cross-module enforcement (token counting/policy + dispatch gate). Unit tests can miss that over-budget requests still get dispatched due to wiring gaps. Rejected System because behavior text is pre-dispatch enforcement, not real external contract validation.
behaviors.test_description: Use real tokenizer/budget policy + dispatcher wiring with a stub client; send over-budget documents and assert policy outcome (truncate or reject) and that dispatch occurs only when post-policy input is within budget.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires live provider-reported usage from a real AI API call; mocks can hide real token-accounting differences. Rejected System as final level because the key dependency is real AI API execution path/behavior, and the behavior is framed around actual provider token usage under model invocation.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real classification calls for representative documents and assert the provider's returned token-usage fields are <= configured per-call budget; fail on any over-budget observation.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is retry orchestration/backoff behavior across client + retry policy modules; deterministic stubs can validate it. Rejected System because the behavior text does not explicitly require validating real external error-contract shape or round-trip side effect, only handling the signal and retrying.
behaviors.test_description: With a controlled clock and stubbed provider sequence (rate-limit signal then success), assert the module waits per policy, retries, and returns successful label instead of surfacing an error.
